output_text:
Subjective: 58-year-old woman reports 3-week history of worsening exertional dyspnea and intermittent palpitations. Denies chest pain, syncope, orthopnea, or paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnea. Past medical history includes hypertension, type 2 diabetes mellitus, and hyperlipidemia. Smoker with 22 pack-year history, quit 3 years ago.

Objective: Vitals show BP 148/92, HR 102 irregular, RR 18, SpO2 96%, BMI 31. Cardiac exam reveals irregularly irregular rhythm with no murmurs; lungs clear bilaterally; trace pretibial edema. ECG shows atrial fibrillation with ventricular rate 98 and no acute ischemic
